sections.heading: 1. Who we are
paragraphs: ChessGuard is an online chess game developed as part of the 42 curriculum. This page describes what data is collected when you use the application and for what reasons.

sections.heading: 2. Data collected
list: Your email address and username, provided at registration.
Your password, stored hashed and salted (never in plain text).
Your game history (moves played, results, opponents) required for the game to work and for your profile statistics.
A session token kept locally in your browser to keep you logged in.

sections.heading: 3. Use of data
paragraphs: This data is used only to run the service: authentication, matching players, saving your games and displaying your profile. It is neither sold, shared with third parties, nor used for advertising purposes.

sections.heading: 4. Retention
paragraphs: Your data is kept as long as your account exists. You can request its deletion at any time by deleting your account or contacting the project team.

sections.heading: 5. Your rights
paragraphs: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify and delete your personal data. To exercise this right, contact the team via the project's GitHub repository.

sections.heading: 6. Cookies and local storage
paragraphs: The application uses the browser's local storage (localStorage) to keep your login token. No tracking or advertising cookies are used.

sections.heading: 1. Purpose
paragraphs: These terms govern the use of ChessGuard, an online chess platform. By creating an account, you accept these terms in their entirety.

sections.heading: 2. Account creation
paragraphs: You must provide a valid email and a username. You are responsible for the confidentiality of your password and for all activity carried out from your account.

sections.heading: 3. Good conduct
list: Fair play is required: using external assistance tools during an online game is prohibited.
Insulting, discriminatory or illegal usernames and messages are not tolerated.
Any attempt to bypass the platform's security results in account suspension.

sections.heading: 4. Game features
paragraphs: The platform offers local games, online games and games against an artificial intelligence. The displayed results and statistics reflect the games actually played on the platform.

sections.heading: 5. Service availability
paragraphs: ChessGuard is a student project provided "as is", without any guarantee of continuous availability. The service may be interrupted or modified at any time, particularly for maintenance.

sections.heading: 6. Account deletion
paragraphs: You can request the deletion of your account at any time. This entails the deletion of your personal data in accordance with our privacy policy.

sections.heading: 7. Contact
paragraphs: For any question regarding these terms, contact the team via the project's GitHub repository.
